actors.text: A randomized experiment finds Drug A reduces blood pressure significantly more than B. Subjects were 60 volunteers. What can we conclude?
Drug A caused lower BP, generalizes to all adults
actors.reflection: Random assignment supports CAUSE, but volunteers are not a random sample of all adults. Cannot generalize beyond similar volunteers.

actors.text: Drug A caused lower BP for these subjects
Drug A is associated with lower BP, no cause
actors.reflection: Random ASSIGNMENT of treatments lets us claim CAUSE inside the experiment. 'Just association' would apply to an observational study, not this one.

completion.rule: any correct QuestionDoor switch pressed by >= 1/3 of online players, then any Player walks onto Goal
